title: #[serde(default)] on UserInfo.sequence has no comment explaining backward-compat intent
description: UserInfo { pub sequence: u64, #[serde(default)] ... } allows deserialization of DirectoryList messages that predate the sequence field — they deserialize to 0 safely. This is intentional for forward compat but there is no comment explaining why the default is 0 and that this is a compat decision, not an omission. A future contributor refactoring UserInfo may remove the default thinking it is unnecessary, breaking deserialization of older relay messages. Add: // default=0: backward compat with pre-sequence relays; offline entries also use 0.
